content: Customer reported Error -6 during image processing on iX1600. Root cause: corrupted ScanSnap Home environment. Resolved via SSHomeClean.exe clean uninstall and reinstall with administrator rights.
Customer reported iX1600 losing USB and Wi-Fi connection repeatedly after firmware update. Root cause: Windows component corruption. Resolved via SFC and DISM repair sequence.

Customer reported unexpected error after firmware update on iX1600. Root cause: firmware update did not complete correctly. Resolved by entering recovery mode (Top Sensor + Empty Arm) and running standalone firmware updater via USB.
Customer reported ScanSnap Home startup crash with tb21 error. Root cause: damaged installation or configuration data. Resolved via cleanup tool and reinstall as administrator.
Customer reported scanner not detected over USB. Root cause: stale USB/ScanSnap device registrations. Resolved by removing old Device Manager entries and rebuilding USB stack.
Customer reported vertical streaks in scanned images. Root cause: dust or contamination on internal glass/roller area. Resolved via careful cleaning of internal glass and roller area.

Customer reported cloud button remaining white after update on iX1600/iX1500. Root cause: cloud token or profile cache issue. Resolved by re-logging into ScanSnap Cloud, recreating cloud profile and reconnecting Wi-Fi.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home unable to save scans to OneDrive. Root cause: cloud authorization or upload state became invalid. Resolved by resending failed cloud jobs and reauthenticating OneDrive account.
Customer reported iX100 failing to reconnect after router change. Root cause: old Wi-Fi settings no longer matched new network. Resolved by removing old Wi-Fi settings and repeating wireless setup.

Customer reported device not responding on iX1500 touch panel. Root cause: profile was tied to wrong computer or communication path. Resolved by recreating local profile on correct computer and verifying network communication.

Customer reported scan button blinking but no scan starting. Root cause: USB hub or security software interfered with scanner communication. Resolved by connecting directly via USB without hub and temporarily disabling antivirus.
Customer reported paper feed skewing on iX1300. Root cause: document loaded at incorrect angle in U-turn or return path. Resolved by realigning documents and testing with Carrier Sheet.
Customer reported ScanSnap Home unable to uninstall correctly. Root cause: leftover installation registry data. Resolved via SSHomeClean.exe Remove Installation Information Tool.

Customer reported imported smartphone PDFs unable to be merged in ScanSnap Home. Root cause: mobile PDF structure incompatible with ScanSnap Home processing. Resolved by converting to compatible PDF/JPG and reimporting via Insert from file.
Customer requested automatic saving without preview window. Root cause: Scan to Folder profile requires confirmation step. Resolved by configuring ScanDirect profile.
Customer reported scanner remaining Ready with no mechanical movement. Root cause: mechanism initialization state stuck. Resolved via power-drain reset and manual feed roller rotation.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home unstable after Windows update with 0x8007045A/0xc000007b/PFUSSMON/FullTrustNotifier errors. Root cause: corrupted Windows runtime/application environment. Resolved via full cleanup/reinstall, Visual C++ repair, SFC/DISM.

Customer reported mobile app scans working but desktop ScanSnap Home not functional. Root cause: desktop-side ScanSnap Home configuration corrupted. Resolved by resetting desktop scanner registration and rebuilding local configuration.

Customer reported scanner disappearing after Windows sleep or hibernate. Root cause: Windows power-state recovery interrupts scanner communication. Resolved by disabling Fast Startup and USB selective suspend.

Customer reported scanner stuck on logo screen after interrupted firmware process on iX1600/iX1500. Root cause: interrupted firmware state prevented normal boot. Resolved via low-level recovery mode via USB and standalone firmware recovery workflow.

Customer reported settings temporarily greyed out and intermittent scanner connection. Root cause: unstable ScanSnap Home service or UI communication. Resolved via SSHomeClean.exe cleanup and fresh installation.

Customer reported scanner connected by USB but ScanSnap Home showing device not connected. Root cause: old USB registrations blocked proper initialization. Resolved by removing stale Device Manager entries and rebuilding USB stack.

Customer reported iX500 firmware updater failing on newer macOS. Root cause: firmware updater compatibility or environment limitation. Resolved using standalone firmware package and compatible workflow.

Customer reported scanner able to scan from device but ScanSnap Home unusable. Root cause: hardware functional but local software environment damaged. Resolved via cleanup/reinstall and reset of local configuration data.

Customer reported cloud profiles stopping after password or account change. Root cause: old cloud authentication tokens invalid. Resolved by removing and recreating ScanSnap Cloud profiles and reauthenticating.

Customer reported orange warning symbol on iX1600 without clear error text. Root cause: profile destination or cloud communication needed attention. Resolved by verifying profile destination and recreating broken cloud profile.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home crashing during OCR processing. Root cause: OCR or image-processing workload/component instability. Resolved by reducing image quality temporarily and rebuilding local application environment.

Customer reported scanner disconnecting during large batch scans. Root cause: USB power management or unstable connection interrupted communication. Resolved by testing direct USB without dock/hub and disabling USB power saving.
Customer reported wireless setup failing on Windows 11 24H2. Root cause: Windows location permission blocking wireless setup. Resolved by enabling Windows location permission for ScanSnap Home.

Customer reported imported files disappearing from ScanSnap Home library. Root cause: external file moves not reflected in ScanSnap Home index. Resolved by refreshing folders and restoring original file locations.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home startup failing after interrupted Windows update. Root cause: damaged Windows component store. Resolved via SFC and DISM repair sequence before reinstalling ScanSnap Home.

Customer reported repeated unexpected error after every reboot. Root cause: broken local user configuration. Resolved by removing corrupted AppData/Roaming/PFU configuration and rebuilding profile environment.

Customer reported Wi-Fi setup failing only on one specific router. Root cause: router configuration blocking scanner communication. Resolved by checking 2.4GHz availability, band steering and MAC filtering.

Customer reported scanner stuck on Connecting in ScanSnap Home. Root cause: broken communication cache or profile state. Resolved by removing and re-adding scanner profile and restarting local services.

Customer reported inability to scan to NAS or network folder. Root cause: network destination path or permission issue. Resolved by testing local folder workflow and rebuilding network destination profile.

Customer reported no email program available during Scan to Email. Root cause: unsupported or missing default desktop mail client. Resolved by verifying default mail application and testing classic Outlook workflow.

Customer reported smartphone-generated PDFs unable to be merged in ScanSnap Home. Root cause: mobile PDF structure incompatible with ScanSnap Home. Resolved by using JPG conversion and reprocessing through ScanSnap Home.

Customer reported scanner working only after reboot and failing again later. Root cause: underlying Windows communication corruption. Resolved via Windows repair sequence and fresh driver/software installation.

Customer reported firmware update prompt repeating although latest firmware was already installed. Root cause: previous firmware update did not finalize correctly. Resolved by repeating firmware installation via direct USB connection only.
Customer reported ScanSnap Home very slow after large library import. Root cause: oversized or corrupted index database. Resolved by reducing active folder scope and refreshing local database/index.

Customer reported scanner visible in Windows but missing in ScanSnap Home. Root cause: broken local device registration. Resolved by removing scanner registration and re-adding device after restarting services.

Customer reported iX1600 displaying white cloud icon and refusing cloud scans. Root cause: cloud session/profile cache corrupted. Resolved by logging out, removing old profile, re-registering scanner and recreating cloud workflow.
Customer reported random save failures only to OneDrive. Root cause: cloud destination integration unstable. Resolved by testing local folder workflow and rebuilding OneDrive integration.

Customer reported same firmware update offered after successful installation. Root cause: firmware installation did not finalize correctly. Resolved by performing firmware update again via direct USB without Wi-Fi.

Customer reported inability to rename scans automatically. Root cause: expected Save As workflow differed from actual profile behavior. Resolved by explaining ScanDirect vs Scan to Folder and adjusting workflow.

Customer suspected hardware defect because scanning stopped completely. Root cause: software environment failure made scanner appear defective. Resolved by testing scanner independently and isolating issue to software environment.
Customer reported ScanSnap Home freezing while loading profiles. Root cause: corrupted profile configuration. Resolved by removing corrupted configuration and recreating profiles manually.
Customer reported all pages identified as blank. Root cause: blank-page detection too aggressive. Resolved by reducing blank-page detection sensitivity and testing grayscale settings.
Customer reported wireless scanning failing but USB working. Root cause: router or Wi-Fi environment unstable. Resolved by reconfiguring wireless setup and checking router isolation/band steering.
Customer reported installer saying product already installed. Root cause: corrupted installation registry entries. Resolved by executing Remove Installation Information Tool and restarting system.

Customer reported scans cropped or cut off unexpectedly. Root cause: automatic size detection or paper alignment issue. Resolved by adjusting paper guides and disabling automatic size detection for testing.
Customer reported ScanSnap Home crashing immediately after launch. Root cause: damaged runtime dependencies. Resolved by repairing Visual C++ runtimes and rebuilding local application environment.
Customer reported custom profiles lost after Windows migration. Root cause: local profile data not migrated correctly. Resolved by recreating profiles and restoring workflow configuration manually.

Customer reported unusual mechanical noise without paper movement. Root cause: mechanism initialization state blocked startup movement. Resolved via power-drain reset and checking feed roller positioning.

Customer reported mobile app working but desktop application failing. Root cause: desktop local environment corrupted. Resolved by resetting desktop-side scanner registration and rebuilding local ScanSnap Home configuration.
Customer reported unstable scanner behavior after sleep or hibernate. Root cause: Windows power management interrupts USB communication. Resolved by disabling USB selective suspend and Fast Startup.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home library empty after folder changes. Root cause: library references pointed to moved or missing folders. Resolved by updating monitored folders and refreshing local database.

Customer reported repeated Wi-Fi pairing failures during setup. Root cause: mixed-band router steering prevented setup. Resolved by temporarily disabling 5GHz steering and configuring on 2.4GHz network.

Customer reported orange LED permanently lit after failed startup. Root cause: low-level initialization or firmware issue. Resolved by collecting photos/videos, verifying service status and preparing firmware recovery/service workflow.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home unstable after importing damaged PDFs. Root cause: corrupted imported files destabilized index/database. Resolved by removing problematic files and rebuilding local index.

Customer reported scanner working once after reinstall then failing again. Root cause: underlying Windows communication corruption persisted. Resolved via deeper SFC/DISM repair and removal of remaining PFU cache data.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home showing offline while scanner display showed ready. Root cause: broken software-side pairing state. Resolved by removing stale scanner pairing and recreating local scanner registration.

Customer reported inability to save files only to network destinations. Root cause: network path or permission instability. Resolved by testing local save and verifying NAS permissions/path stability.

Customer reported scans becoming slower over time. Root cause: local indexing/cache buildup reduces performance. Resolved by clearing temporary files, refreshing monitored folders and rebuilding local index cache.
Customer reported Wi-Fi setup disappearing during pairing. Root cause: initial wireless negotiation unstable. Resolved by connecting temporarily via USB and repeating wireless configuration.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home repeatedly requesting login. Root cause: corrupted account/session configuration. Resolved by removing stored account configuration and recreating local authentication setup.

Customer reported scanner endlessly initializing after startup. Root cause: startup position or mechanical initialization stuck. Resolved via power-drain reset and manual check of feed mechanism movement.

Customer reported scans rotated incorrectly. Root cause: automatic orientation detection misinterpreted document layout. Resolved by disabling automatic rotation and testing manual orientation settings.
Customer reported scanner failing only on one Windows user profile. Root cause: user-profile-specific ScanSnap data corrupted. Resolved by testing with a newly created Windows user profile.

Customer reported scanner not detected after antivirus update. Root cause: security software blocking scanner communication components. Resolved by temporarily disabling security software and recreating local scanner communication.
Customer reported searchable PDFs unable to be created. Root cause: OCR configuration or environment corrupted. Resolved by verifying OCR settings and rebuilding OCR-related application components.

Customer reported long batch jobs cancelling repeatedly. Root cause: USB power saving interrupts long-running communication. Resolved by disabling USB power saving and testing direct motherboard USB ports.

Customer reported application unstable after importing thousands of files. Root cause: oversized indexing workload destabilized library. Resolved by reducing monitored folders and refreshing database incrementally.
Customer reported unexpected error only with specific profiles. Root cause: affected profile configuration corrupted. Resolved by recreating affected profiles manually from scratch.

Customer reported cloud scans working on phone but failing from touch panel on iX1600/iX1500. Root cause: scanner/profile cloud authorization invalid. Resolved by reauthenticating cloud account and recreating cloud-linked profile.

Customer reported JPEG scans working but PDF generation failing. Root cause: PDF/image-processing component corrupted. Resolved by reducing processing load and rebuilding PDF-related application components.
Customer reported scanner disappearing after Windows wake-up. Root cause: power-state recovery interrupts communication. Resolved by disabling Fast Startup and USB selective suspend.
Customer reported random profile duplication and missing shortcuts. Root cause: local configuration data corrupted. Resolved by removing damaged profile cache and recreating profiles manually.

Customer reported inability to reconnect after replacing router hardware. Root cause: stored network configuration no longer matched router. Resolved by resetting wireless settings and repeating full Wi-Fi pairing process.

Customer reported iX1600/iX1500 completely undetected after interrupted firmware installation. Root cause: low-level firmware state prevents normal detection. Resolved via model-specific USB firmware recovery workflow; escalated if recovery failed.

Customer reported Wi-Fi connected but scans never arriving in ScanSnap Home. Root cause: network isolation prevented scan delivery. Resolved by rebuilding scanner registration and verifying computer/scanner same subnet.

Customer reported ScanSnap Home closing during scan processing. Root cause: corrupted temporary PFU processing cache. Resolved by removing temporary PFU cache folders and reinstalling latest ScanSnap Home.
Customer reported preview thumbnails remaining blank. Root cause: thumbnail/index database corrupted. Resolved by refreshing monitored folders and rebuilding local content index.

Customer reported feed rollers rotating briefly but paper not being pulled in. Root cause: roller seating or initialization state prevents feed start. Resolved by checking roller seating and performing initialization reset.

Customer reported firmware update disconnecting mid-process. Root cause: unstable USB communication interrupts firmware transfer. Resolved by repeating update via direct motherboard USB without hubs/extensions.

Customer reported scans appearing washed out or low contrast. Root cause: aggressive automatic correction distorted output. Resolved by resetting profile image settings and disabling automatic correction temporarily.
Customer reported scanner detection lost after moving to docking station. Root cause: docking station USB instability. Resolved by testing scanner directly on native USB ports.
Customer reported scanner busy although no active scan existed. Root cause: background communication session locked. Resolved by restarting ScanSnap background services and rebooting scanner.

Customer reported scanner disappearing intermittently on Wi-Fi. Root cause: changing IP assignments caused unstable communication. Resolved by assigning fixed DHCP reservation and rebuilding wireless profile.

Customer reported single pages scanning but batch jobs failing. Root cause: profile/image-processing overload destabilizes long jobs. Resolved by reducing processing load and testing simplified profile.

Customer reported scanned PDFs opening corrupted or unreadable. Root cause: corrupted output configuration generated invalid PDFs. Resolved by testing alternate save destination and recreating affected profile.
Customer reported ScanSnap Home freezing when switching profiles. Root cause: broken configuration cache caused UI instability. Resolved by removing damaged profile cache and recreating profiles.

Customer reported inability to reconnect after Microsoft account credential change. Root cause: stored authentication tokens invalid. Resolved by reauthenticating linked cloud/account services and rebuilding mappings.

Customer reported scanner LED blinking continuously without scanning. Root cause: unstable communication prevented scan initialization. Resolved by testing alternate USB cable and removing power-management restrictions.
Customer reported scans failing only when OCR enabled. Root cause: OCR processing components corrupted. Resolved by disabling OCR temporarily and rebuilding local OCR environment.

Customer reported duplicate scans after interrupted communication. Root cause: communication retries triggered repeated processing. Resolved by rebuilding profile workflow and resetting communication state.

Customer reported inability to save scans after company security policy update. Root cause: updated security policy blocked destination access. Resolved by verifying folder permissions and testing unrestricted local save path.

Customer reported scanner startup extremely slow after software update. Root cause: legacy cached environment data slowed initialization. Resolved by clearing old application cache and refreshing scanner registration.
Customer reported scanner working only in Windows Safe Mode. Root cause: third-party background software interfering. Resolved via clean boot diagnostics and isolation of startup conflicts.

Customer reported iX1600/iX1500 completely undetected after interrupted firmware installation. Root cause: low-level firmware recovery required. Resolved via model-specific USB firmware recovery workflow; escalated only if recovery failed.
